file_insights.changes: Added summary statistics display at end of list output
functions_modified: list_cards() - added call to calculate_stats() and print_summary()

patterns_discovered.description: Converts seconds to human-readable format (e.g., '4m32s'). Rounds partial seconds and omits zero-second component when minutes are present
Only displays state counts > 0 to avoid clutter; uses HashMap lookup with fallback
patterns_discovered.code: if let Some(&count) = stats.by_state.get(state) { if count > 0 { ... } }

patterns_discovered.description: Stats joined with ' · ' separator for readability: '14 total · 2 running · 8 done · 1 failed · avg 4m32s'
Average duration only included if present (Some value), using Option<f64> pattern

gotchas_discovered.note: Function directly prints to stdout; test comment acknowledges this limitation: 'we're just verifying it doesn't panic'

gotchas_discovered.example: 5.7 seconds rounds to 6s; test explicitly covers this

gotchas_discovered.issue: Zero-padding not used in duration display
gotchas_discovered.note: Displays '4m32s' not '4m32s' with zero-padding; acceptable for human readability

approach_details.result: Implemented on first attempt without revision
approach_details.test_strategy: Added 3 focused unit tests covering duration formatting (seconds/minutes edge cases) and summary display
approach_details.completeness: Feature fully implemented with comprehensive test coverage

recommendations.suggestion: Refactor print_summary() to return String instead of printing directly
recommendations.benefit: Improves testability and allows caller to format output (e.g., add colors, redirect to file)

recommendations.suggestion: Add capture test for print_summary output
recommendations.benefit: Current test only verifies no panic; could verify exact output format
recommendations.note: Requires gag crate or similar for stdout capture

recommendations.suggestion: Consider state display order as configuration
recommendations.benefit: Hardcoded order (running, done, failed, pending) works now but may need flexibility later
